page: Amazon Web Services m5a.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7571 processor based on the x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The instance provides 32 virtual CPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance tradeoff between single-core and multi-core workloads. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core CPU, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded compression tests all scoring in the bottom 10% of tested hardware. Conversely, multi-core performance is more robust, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores landing in the middle 50% average tier. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached reads and writes also perform in the average tier.

Given its hardware profile and performance characteristics, the m5a.8xlarge is suited for general-purpose workloads that can leverage parallel processing rather than high single-thread performance. Appropriate applications include multi-threaded database operations, static web serving, Redis key-value stores, and software compilation. It can handle light machine learning tasks, such as prompt processing and text generation with small 135M models, though performance drops to the bottom 25% for medium 7B models. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a balanced allocation of compute and memory resources for parallelized enterprise workloads, though workloads requiring high single-core throughput or local NVMe storage should look elsewhere.
description: Amazon Web Services m5a.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor on the x86_64 architecture. Managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it features 32 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance does not include local storage and delivers an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks indicate a significant tradeoff: single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance are in the bottom 10%, while multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average middle 50% tier. This resource profile makes the m5a.8xlarge cost-efficient for parallelized workloads such as multi-threaded web serving, Redis operations, and software compilation, whereas it is less suitable for single-thread intensive applications or large-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5a.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7571 CPU. It offers average multi-core performance but weak single-core throughput.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5a.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server offering thirty-two vCPUs and average multi-core performance for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
Equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Delivers average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Exhibits lower single-core and single-threaded compression performance
Suited for multi-threaded databases, static web serving, and Redis operations
Does not include local SSD storage or hardware GPU accelerators
